entry.title: Re: Restful ABAP Object Page #LINEITEM_REFERENCE

entry.title: Re: Credit Exposure Update
entry.content: Hi @pgunda79, Which approach did you follow. Did you face any issue with the selected approach later?

entry.title: Custom Field not available in SAP Standart ODATA API Activated
entry.content:
Hi Guys!

We have added a Custom Field "Link" to vf03 transaction, Billing Document Header Details.

We have activated the standard Billing Document ODATA https://api.sap.com/api/sap-s4-CE_BILLINGDOCUMENT_0001-v1/resource/Header,  but the Custom Field "Link" is no appearing in the metadata.

We need this field to be available in this ODATA Standart https://api.sap.com/api/sap-s4-CE_BILLINGDOCUMENT_0001-v1/resource/Header that we have activated.

 

=>> Attachment below.

Please, any tips?

 

Thanks a lot!!!

entry.title: Producer Sync Issues - Unsorted and Local Trash
entry.content:
Hello, 

My team just notified me that starting 12/16/24, when they open producer and sync their workspace, certain objects (either a full course, book page, simulation) are going to either their Unsorted Folder or Local Trash or from Unsorted to Local Trash. 

I just did a test on my side and synced my workarea and a developer confirmed that an object prior to me syncing wasn't in the trash but now it is.

Has anyone else faced this issue?

entry.title: Re: Date prefilled as Yesterday in Calender
entry.content: So it will filter out the data for the date as yesterday? or 3 days before?

entry.title: Re: Date prefilled as Yesterday in Calender
entry.content: basically a calender should be there as story filter, and once the date is selected it should go back and filter previous day if its any other day than monday and it should show up friday data filtered out, if the date is selected as monday.

entry.title: Consuming a released event of an unreleased RAP BO (how to achieve clean core)
entry.content:
Hi,

Currently we are confused. I was advised that the events in the accelerator hub can be consumed either via local or remote event consumption.

https://api.sap.com/event/OP_PRODUCTEVENTS/overview

Currently, local event consumption applies to RAP BO events. So I searched for the released RAP BO for products (https://api.sap.com/bointerface/PCE_I_PRODUCTTP_2) and tried to create and implement and event handler class.

First thing that I noticed is that the events being raised are not there in the released RAP BO.

After further checking, I saw that the event is actually defined at the base BO level.

I did some further testing and found out that these events are being from the Manage Product Master fiori app by calling these static methods which in turn raises the RAP BO events.

Now, when I tried to consume these events using the released RAP BO, the system won't allow me because the events are defined at the base BO level. We are using S/4HANA 2023 private cloud edition (FPS00). We are not sure if the same behavior is there in the FPS version.

When I checked the SAP documentation(https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-rap/business-event-consumption), it says that the event to be consumed needs to be exposed in a C1 released RAP BO interface.

However, the events are not exposed for most of the released RAP BOs interfaces (e.g., Product, Business Partner, Pricing). For these 3 RAP BOs, the events are only exposed in the base RAP BO.

The only way we can consume this is to create the event handler class under a 'Standard ABAP' package so that it will allows us to consume the event from the unreleased base RAP BO. We are trying to achieve clean core and we are limiting any Tier 3 developments. Is there a way to consume these events via Tier 1 approach without resorting to using SAP event mesh? SAP event mesh is usually only recommended if the event producer and consumer is different but in our case, the producer/consumer is the same S4H system.

Note: We are trying to use these events to replace the BTE functionality.

entry.title: Re: NF-e Nota Técnica 2023.004 v1.10: novos campos adicionados à nota fiscal
entry.content: Boa tarde! Qual é a badi/função/enhancement que deve ser incluída a lógica de preenchimento do campo INDDEDUZDESON, que agora existe na tela do item da nota fiscal por favor ? Essa função? Essa é uma alternativa J_1BNF_MERGE_BADI_FIELDS? Obrigada!

entry.title: Re: AMDP Select with TOP 3 entries based on certain fields
entry.content:
There is no "group by vendor". It's only sorted by DMBTR (descending).

About ROW_NUMBER(), RANK(), and DENSE_RANK() Funct... - SAP Community (ROW_NUMBER() OVER(order by ...) ROW_num where ROW_num <= 3)

entry.title: Re: NF-e Nota Técnica 2023.004 v1.10: novos campos adicionados à nota fiscal
entry.content:
@luize_boyen @lipe_flores , acabei de fazer a pergunta acima. As notas já foram aplicadas e na função mencionada eu tenho a seguinte linha de código:  ls_nflin-inddeduzdeson = ls_nflin_badi-inddeduzdeson. "3423950.

Obrigada!

entry.title: Condition Contract for Sales - Manual Delta Accruals
entry.content:
Hi gurus,

we have implemented the Condition Contract for sales in an S/4HANA Project, and introduced the manual delta accrual document to manage both data migration and unplanned delta accruals.

While we are able to post manual delta accruals correctly using the RENT pricing condition (the CC is also updated inserting a new date in the settlement calenar), we encounter errors when issuing follow-on documents:

  1. Without specifying the RENT condition in the Condition Type Group for Accruals (as per standard configuration):

    • Automatic delta accruals are processed correctly.
    • However, the amount booked as "manual delta accrual" is not reversed during the final settlement run.

  2. With RENT condition specified in the Condition Type Group for Accruals (RENT condition type assigned to "Tgt AccReversal" as REA2 and "Tgt CType Daccr" as RED2):

    • The first automatic delta accrual reverses the amount booked as "manual delta accrual."

Could you please provide guidance on how to resolve these issues?

Thank you

Michele

entry.title: Re: Credit Management: Is there a TCode for orders releas...
entry.content:
Try ME2L (or ME2M) with Selection parameters such as WE02

  • You can create new selection parameters within Customizing: Purchasing, Reporting, Maintain Purchasing Lists, Selection Parameters, Define Selection Parameters (SM30 V_160S)

entry.title: Re: Credit Management: Is there a TCode for orders released but not invoiced yet?
entry.content: Ok for me, you could also add MM Purchasing

entry.title: Re: CJI3 - SUBMIT RKPEP003 doesn't work with parameter
entry.content: There are two parameters by type of CO item report (actual, planned, etc.)

entry.title: Re: solr admin page connection refused after upgrading to...

very useful information there. Thanks for sharing.

Partners can also reach out to us at icc@sap.com.

Thanks, Imran

entry.title: Re: Locked by another user (myself)

How can I complete the receiving for the remaining line item .It was It ticket  created from Business side.

Thanks,

Janmeet

entry.title: Authorizations for SAP Business Technology Plattform - a quick start
entry.content:
1. Introduction to Clean Core:

For SAP customers, hybrid authorization concepts are especially vital under the SAP clean core strategy. With this approach the aim is to keep the core SAP systems lean, minimizing customizations for easier maintenance and updates: 

Having a closer look into the so-called side – by – side extensions, we recognize the following typical architecture: 

Business Data is transferred to SAP BTP (e.g. using SAP Smart Data Access – SAP SDA / SAP Smart Data Integration – SAP SDI) and stored in SAP HANA Cloud as part of a CAP Application running on SAP Business Technology Platform. 

Such an architecture requires protection on two different levels: 

1. Structuring the access to the apps via SAP Build Workzone Standard Edition Roles 

2. Restricting the access on business data level (e.g. display / change, restrict to dedicated company codes, plants, …) 

In this blogpost, we will focus on the second restriction level. 

2. Overview of SAP BTP Levels and their Authorization Management 

To understand the authorization management on SAP BTP, it is important to know the setup of the platform and its levels. When managing authorization on the levels, each is handled separately, and permissions are granted independently. For example, a role on a global account level will not be available in subaccounts. 

On each level below, predefined role collections can be granted. A role collection is a set of individual roles that includes permissions for various resources and services on SAP BTP. It can contain one or multiple roles. 

Global Account: 

The Global Account is the highest level in SAP BTP's organizational structure. 

It represents the contractual boundary of your SAP BTP subscription. On this level it is possible to create custom role collections besides the predefined role collections. 

Directory: 

Directories can optionally be used to group subaccounts for better organization and shared resource management. 

Subaccount: 

Subaccounts are created within the Global Account and are used to manage entitlements, quotas, and assign services specific to each subaccount. On this level it is also possible to create custom role collections besides the predefined role collections. The subaccount level is the most important level for authorization management, as you can create custom roles to protect business applications.  

Org (Organization): 

In the context of Cloud Foundry environments, an Org is used to manage and isolate resources. 

Space: 

Spaces are created within Orgs. It is a logical container that is used for app development, deployment and maintenance. 

3. Authorization Management on the Subaccount Level – Creating a custom role  

Creating custom roles for business applications at the subaccount level requires interaction across three domains. Here’s a step-by-step guide to help you understand this process: 

3.1 Provision Role Templates by SAP BTP Developer (grey box) 

The first step involves setting up role templates, which are provided by the SAP BTP Developer. These templates can be tailored within the customer’s business application based on existing attributes and scope. 

  • Scope: Defines the actions an app needs to execute specific functions, such as editing, viewing, or deleting. 
  • Attributes: Used to specify static or instance-based authorizations that vary depending on the context, such as location, department, or cost center. Attributes help refine business users' authorizations, particularly concerning organizational levels like plants or company codes. 

3.2 User Account and Authentication (UAA) Level managed by SAP BTP Authorization Administrator (blue box)

At this stage, role templates transform into roles and role collections. Here's how it works: 

  • A role directly corresponds to a created role template. 
  • A single role template can inform multiple roles by creating roles with different attributes. 
  • These roles need to be assigned to one or more role collections. A role collection can encompass multiple roles, enhancing flexibility in user permissions. 

3.3 Assigning Roles to Users by User Administrator (green box) 

  • Direct Assignment: Assign one or more role collections directly to users on the User Account and Authentication level. 
  • Group Assignment: Assign one or more role collections to user groups. Note that roles cannot be directly assigned to individual users or user groups. 

By following these steps, you can effectively create and manage custom roles for business applications, ensuring that users have the appropriate permissions to perform their tasks efficiently. 

4. Quick Start Service and Tool Support (by SAP Consulting)

The role concept for SAP Business Technology Platform can become complex quickly, if business data is stored as part of a CAP Application. Just let us validate the following (fictive) example: 

A medium / large size customer plans to cover his plant maintenance processes via a clean core approach and requires three extensions which are developed on SAP Business Technology Platform. All three applications have a simple authorization concept consisting of an admin role, an expert role and a display role, so we have overall 9 functional roles available. Decentralized access is required for 20 plants, so the security team has to manage 180 BTP roles. 

The SAP Service provides the following features: 

  • Knowledge Transfer Workshop for BTP Authorizations 

Learn how the Authorizations on SAP BTP are managed. Get a Deep Dive on the BTP Levels, Role Collections and Roles and Role Templates.  

  • A best practice basis role concept for a quick start of the SAP BTP development project 

The customer can utilize the best practice role templates provided by SAP to get a quick start on their project. This will help them quickly structure their role management for BTP operations, BTP security, BTP developer and tester. 

  • AIRC BTP Role Collection Generator 

- UI5 User Interface: 

The UI5 interface will provide the customer with an user-friendly way to manage and track their roles and role collections, making it easier for the authorization team to monitor and adjust access controls as needed. 

- Download Roles and Role Collections via Excel: 

The customer’s security team can use this feature to export their role and role collection configurations into Excel. This is beneficial for documentation, audits, and for backup purposes.

- Update Roles and Role Collections via Excel: 

With this feature, the customer will be able to make bulk updates to their roles and role collections through Excel. This is useful for managing changes efficiently, especially when adjustments need to be made across multiple roles or role collections.

- Transfer Roles and Role Collections via Excel across subaccounts: 

With this feature it is possible to transport roles and role collections across the system landscape (dev, qas, prd) as long there is no integration into transport management. Another use case is to transfer your basis role concept across different subaccounts.

If you are interested in this service, please get in contact with the authors of this blogpost.

5. Introduction to Authorization Management Service (AMS) 

Authorization Management Service (AMS) is a new, improved method to develop and manage user authorizations for SAP Business Technology Platform applications in a centralized way. This blogpost will not further focus on details of this service, but conclude with the general recommendations which technology to choose in which situation: 

  • For new developments, the usage of AMS is recommended 
  • It is not mandatory to update existing developments to AMS. However, using AMS can benefit your overall authorization management posture in BTP and help manage BTP authorizations more effectively. Unfortunately, there is no automatic migration to AMS possible so that developments must be adapted, and policies must be created and distributed “manually”. 

Currently, the Role Collection Generator just covers the classical approach. With the availability of corresponding interfaces, the role collection generator will also cover AMS. 

The following source provide some further information on AMS: 

  • Configuring Authorization Policies 
  • Assign Authorization Policies 
  • AMS in the CAP “Golden Path” documentation 

6. Wrapping it up – What are the key takeaways? 

Hybrid authorizations are crucial for SAP solution extensions in the context of the SAP clean core strategy. SAP BTP involves multiple levels where authorizations are managed separately. Custom roles and role collections can be created and assigned at the subaccount level, ensuring appropriate user permissions. Tools like the AIRC Role Collection Generator and Authorization Management Service streamline role management and enhance security. If you want to learn more about SAP S/4HANA authorization concepts, please visit our first blog.

entry.title: Word Table tags For Accessibility
entry.content:
For accessibility purposes we may be required to add tags to the Word based schedules/tables In Disclosure Management.

There is a Word feature for table properties > alt text tab title and description fields. These are overwritten when the data links are refreshed (as expected).
Any other ideas for labeling the tables?

Maybe the XBRL tags? Not familiar with their usage.

Regards,
Eyal

entry.title: Re: Possible Issue with SAP Fiori Generator v1.16.0 (ODat...
entry.content: Thank you, JungHeeChan and saurabh__khare, for sharing the solution! Downgrading with the suggested command worked perfectly for me as well. Much appreciated!

entry.title: Re: How to Link SRGBTBREL-BRELGUID with SOFFPHIO-PHIO_ID????
entry.content:
Hi,
We would like to get the information based on SOFFCONT1 PHIO_ID. However the note describes only option to identify the PHIO_ID from SRGBTBREL table. 
I am looking for option to establish relation between SOFFCONT1-PHIO_ID with SRGBTBREL-BRELGUID.
Any suggestions will be really helpful!!
Thanks and Regards,

Vignesh Sridhar.

entry.title: Re: Date prefilled as Yesterday in Calender
entry.content: these lines of code just set the default day offset for the story current date picker, then the data filtering is just based how the time range filter defined which is associated with this current date picker .

entry.title: Deep Link for Completed Forms goes to Inbox
entry.content:
We use a deep link to the completed forms folder (sf/completed-comp-forms) in our completed notifications. Post 2H release, we have noticed that the previously working link takes us to the forms inbox instead.

Has anyone else experienced this behavior? Is there a solution to have the deep link navigate to the completed forms unfiled folder which is the standard SF folder?

__PRESENT

entry.title: Re: Download Process Flows SAP Signavio Process Insights
entry.content:
Hi @CP_PL 

One addition to the answer of my colleague @Veronika_: Downloading the abovementioned content will be a feature request and a ticket via SAP for Me is required to proceed. Regarding your second question, you may also check out the user guide to see what process flows are in the End-to-End Processes and Transformation Planning.

Kind regards

Ascha

entry.title: TIME_OUT Error in FBL3N and FAGLL03 Transactions After Upgrade to S/4HANA
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support,

We are encountering a recurring TIME_OUT error when executing the transactions FBL3N and FAGLL03 after upgrading our SAP system from ECC6 to S/4HANA. These transaction (FBL3N) worked correctly in our previous ECC6 environment, but they fail to execute in S/4HANA.

Details of the Issue:

  1. Transaction Name: FBL3N / FAGLL03
  2. System Version:
    • Previous: SAP ECC 6.0
    • Current: SAP S/4HANA
  3. Selection Criteria Used:
    • Date range: [e.g., 01.01.2023 to 31.12.2023]
    • Accounts: [e.g.,3*]
    • Other filters: [e.g., company code 1000].
    • Line items selection :
    • Status : All Line Items
  4. Error Details:
    • Error: TIME_OUT
    • Program: [e.g., SAPLFBL3 or SAPMF05L].

Best regards

entry.title: Re: Attachments in mobile services Can anyone help me?
entry.content: Most likely the service will provide a REST API you can use.  You would need to create a destination in Mobile Services pointing to this service with the necessary security/authentication.  In the MDK app you can use the SendRequest action or clientAPI call to then interact with the REST API.  Since the API is defined by the service you will need to look into their documentation for details of what and how to call it.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative voucher
entry.content:
@David Chaviano 

Could you kindly assist in getting the vouchers for them for the free attempt?

I would greatly appreciate your help with this matter.

Thank you in advance for your assistance!

entry.title: Re: Relaxed Flow not working in Work Center POD
entry.content:
Hi,

With Relaxed Flow ON, you can have a QTY > 1 SFC active in multiple operations at the same time.

With Relaxed Flow OFF, you can´t start the next operation until the whole qty is completed in the previous operation.

Hope it helps,
Manoel

entry.title: Re: Getting Started with ABAP: How to Create Help View and Search Help Part 1
entry.content: Informative post for beginners like me

entry.title: Re: Code List based on CDS View doesn't show View Entitie...
entry.content:
I answer myself.

It seems the paging on the Custom Entity class must be handled in a different way when the caller is a value help. Or at least that's my understanding.

I had to call following method before performing paging:

io_response->set_total_number_of_records( LINES( lt_FreightCode_tab ) ).

By doing this, the Value Help now shows the correct total number of entries at the top and performs the paging correctly when I scroll down.

entry.title: Re: SAP SuccessFactors Learning System options for practice
entry.content:
Hi @LeahL , 

The type of permission that you are looking for corresponds to the Learning Management (LMS) module, which is not included in the THR80 - Exploring SAP SuccessFactors Platform, but it would be in scope for the THR88 - Configuring SAP SuccessFactors Learning. 

If you are following this learning journey https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/explore-the-sap-successfactors-platform the Learning System Access is THR80

If you are following this learning journey https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/configuring-sap-successfactors-learning the Learning System Access is THR88

Hope this will help, 

Regards, 

Jordi

entry.title: Re: Attachments in mobile services Can anyone help me?
entry.content: @Bill Froelich, Hi thanks I have already tried but I get error, (HTTP status on the back is 412), I was hoping to find documentation on how to set up the action sendrequest or another way to get the image sent when it is captured by the phone, maybe you know another way to do it other than the one mentioned in the message?

entry.title: Re: fb60 posting error:‘format for reference document number is invalid’
entry.content: In SAP S4 HANA 2023, the new path is as follow

entry.title: Empowering AI Innovation: How Software Partners benefit from SAP Business AI
entry.content:
SAP’s software partners are essential in delivering innovative solutions and driving accelerated roadmaps for customers. Generative AI continues to maintain strong momentum in the market, creating exciting opportunities for our software partners: with the power of SAP Business AI, partners are uniquely positioned to address diverse customer needs and foster business growth.

AI technology is rapidly evolving with the potential to reshape industries globally, increasing a projected economic impact of $4.1 trillion (the figure is comparable Germany's annual GDP!) over the next three years. As AI becomes more widely adopted, solutions without AI capabilities risk being obsolete. While challenges remain, enterprise ready AI solutions already create immense customer value.

SAP partners differentiate by integrating AI capabilities into their solutions and thereby transforming decision-making, automate processes, and enhance customer experiences. For example:

Sodales for Enterprise Health, Safety, and Employee Relations (SAP Endorsed App) leverages AI to streamline incident reporting and policy management, improving workplace safety and compliance.

“Developing on SAP's GenAI Hub allows us to leverage cutting edge AI technologies within SAP Business Technology Platform, enabling faster innovation, scalable solutions, and a competitive edge in the AI-driven market.” Sana Salam, CEO of Sodales Solutions

The Greenwashing Detector uses AI to analyze corporate disclosures and social media, detecting misleading sustainability claims and enhancing transparency within SAP Sustainability Control Tower.

“The GenAI hub is a gamechanger for us in the SAP universe. SAP acts as an AI orchestrator, leveraging SAP BTP's capabilities.” Peter Umscheid, CEO of msg global solutions

Agil AI TimeManager utilizes AI for time tracking, scheduling, and labor compliance, boosting productivity and reducing costs within the SAP ecosystem.

“SAP GenAI Hub on SAP BTP unites AI, GenAI, and LL Models with powerful SAP BTP technologies and top development tools, streamlining innovation with a single contract.“ Thomas Neuhaus, Head of Digital Experience Services at Agilita

These solutions are all powered by the SAP BTP Generative AI Hub as the technology backbone: With the BTP GenAI Hub, SAP offers straightforward technical and commercial access to a diverse array of Large Language Models (LLMs), enabling businesses to choose the best model that suits their specific needs. Businesses benefit from the flexibility of not being restricted to a single LLM provider, which allows them to adapt to changing needs and opt for providers that offer the most advanced solutions. 

The SAP Generative AI Hub comes equipped with features like AI Lifecycle Management including prompt templating, data masking, and input filtering, enhancing the orchestration of AI capabilities, and ensuring more secure and user-friendly application development.

How to get started?

The potential for partner innovations in AI is vast, from predictive maintenance and demand forecasting to personalized marketing and optimized supply chain management. As AI continues to evolve, opportunities for SAP partners will further expand. 

To allow for a fast start, we have launched the AI Build Acceleration Package

The incentive accompanies the creation and promotion of your AI solutions, complete with expert support and community resources. Don’t miss out on commercial benefits such as visibility features and the AI certification:

Register now to start leveraging SAP's Business AI platform and bring your innovative AI use cases to life.

For additional information, visit:

  • SAP Partner Portal Pages
  • Explore SAP Business AI on SAP PartnerEdge
  • SAP Business AI
  • SAP Business Technology Platform
  • SAP Partner Benefits Catalog – AI Build Acceleration Package

For further questions, feedback or co-innovation ideas please feel free to reach out to GenAIpartners@sap.com

entry.title: Re: Merit calculation to be to be based on Custom Current...
entry.content:
Hi @swagatikapanda 

No you can only use a custom field with ID = meritTarget (and you also have to switch a tag at the bottom of the XML to “Target”) and import the data from EC for it or import it through UDF if no EC. That field cannot be controlled by a formula so no lookup table possible. Configuration examples here.

Another option is to use compounding which allows us to base the merit increase off of merit + another recommendation column for example (extra). 

All the best,

Xavier

entry.title: Re: fb60 posting error:‘format for reference document number is invalid’
entry.content: SIMG--Cross Application Componenet---General Application Function---Document Reporting Compliance---Country/Region Specific Setting---Argentina---Statutory Reporting---Last Official Document Number---General Setting----Assign Document Class to Document Type

entry.title: Re: Configuration Transport Center - Preparing Transport Request from Source tenant
entry.content:
I have a few questions about this process.

  • If I want to put the system's default messages in the bundle, is it possible to do everything at once?
  • If I put a configuration in the Bundle, every time I make a change to the object do I have to put it back in the Bundle?
  • Can I put all the configuration in the same bundle? Is this a good practice? For example, everything related to Time Management (Time Types, Workflows, Time Profile, Rules...)

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Difference between GR/IR control & GR/IR Indicator in...
entry.content:
• GR/IR clearing
    This function clears the Purchases in Transit and Unbilled Payables accounts when the goods received and the associated invoices are recorded. It determines any differences between the values of the goods receipts and the invoices. 
     
  • GR/IR indicator
    If the GR/IR indicator is set to mandatory in the PO field selection group GR/IR Control, the error "Enter GR non-valuated" will be raised.

entry.title: Re: Any object to change prices of shipping costs? (ie a BAPI for VI02)
entry.content: I think we will use the V54B0001 enhancement (SMOD): we'll try to create the shipping cost via BI and then use an EXIT to add the conditions we will need. Wish me luck!

entry.title: Re: Variable Pay live profile view required in Employee l...
entry.content:
Hi @rkumar1947 

  • If the template Default Currency view (in Plan Setup > Settings) is "User" (recommended) then we should check the following setting in XML : liveprofile-indiv-currency-view and make sure it is set to 1 which aligns the individual view with the User default currency view and can prevent questions from employees and managers when they access the individual view.

https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blogs-by-sap/compensation-checklist-before-launching-a-new-cycle/ba-p/13572980

All the best

Xavier

entry.title: Re: CAP Request Builder converts Number into String
entry.content: Have u got any solution for that?

entry.title: Product Hierarchy For Procurement in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello,

On ME51N, I am trying to select a material using the Product Hierarchy function. When I click on the material match code and search for a product by Product Hierarchy, the system displays the nodes of the created and activated Product hierarchies but does not show any materials. The search result returns Zero.

Is there a step I'm missing?

What should I do te resolve this issue?

Thank you for your help!

entry.title: Re: "Delivery completed" indicator causes approved Purchase order to be re-sent for approv
entry.content: Would you be able to share the screenshots from SPRO for Manage conditions screen and also Fiori workflow ?

entry.title: From Snail Mail to Speedy Billing
entry.content:
In today’s fast-paced digital transformation landscape, it is imperative for enterprises to adopt agile and robust solutions that enhance their operational efficiency. SAP has been leading customers' digital transformations across industries for over 50 years. While our solutions cover most customer needs, we also rely on a strong partner ecosystem to complete end-to-end processes. How does SAP manage this? What if a centuries-old postal service could show us the way?

The Transformation Journey of CTT Correios de Portugal

Let’s dive into the story of CTT Correios de Portugal and see how they went from old-school mail delivery to cutting-edge billing innovation. SAP Convergent Mediation by Digital Route, an extension of SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management (BRIM), is transforming the way businesses handle their billing.

Originally focused on mail delivery, the rise of digital technologies pushed CTT Correios to evolve into the parcel distribution sector. To accomplish this, they needed a powerful billing system to support their new business model.

How Did SAP Convergent Mediation Help?

From its inception in 2016 and successful go-live in April 2019, the implementation of SAP BRIM and SAP Convergent Mediation has enabled CTT Correios to revolutionize their billing processes, particularly within their parcel distribution sector. Below are key takeaways from their journey:

  • Enhancing Data Quality: SAP Convergent Mediation by Digital Route acts as a filter that processes and sorts approximately 200,000 events daily, subsequently integrating around 70,000 events into SAP for invoicing. This rigorous filtering ensures only reliable data proceeds further into the system.
  • Handling High Volumes and Complex Pricing: For CTT, the mediation tool  processes high transaction volumes, enriched data and corrected errors, thereby preventing data quality issues that plagued their previous system.
  • Real-time Integration: One of the significant benefits that CTT experienced was the real-time integration of information, which allowed them to move from hourly updates to immediate information flow. This made their operations speedy not only for internal customers but also enhanced the overall billing process efficiency.
  • Revenue Assurance: Revenue leakage was a big pain point for CTT. Just like a drip in a faucet can waste gallons of water over time, small issues in billing data can lead to significant revenue losses. SAP Convergent Mediation ensures every bit of data is accurate, preventing revenue leakage and ensuring every dollar is accounted for.
  • Scalability and Future-readiness: As they plan to move to the cloud, CTT needed a system that could enable growth. The scalability feature of SAP Convergent Mediation became crucial to support future expansion and integration with new business units, making it an integral part of CTT’s long-term strategy.

A Step Forward in Digital Transformation

Deploying SAP Convergent Mediation as an extension of BRIM has evidently yielded transformative results for CTT Correios de Portugal. Their ability to quickly adapt to new business requirements and the seamless integration of convergent mediation for billing processes represents a remarkable milestone in their digital evolution.

This transformative story demonstrates how customers in the public sector and other industries can benefit from the wide ecosystem of industry partner solutions that complement and extend SAP’s robust portfolio of solutions.

For further information, feel free to explore SAP Convergent Mediation by Digital Route and other public sector-specific solutions from our partners.

entry.title: Error after Inbound delivery creation in SMQ2 - PDI_HDR_029 , message class : /BOPF/FRW
entry.content:
Hi everyone, 

After inbound delivery creation in S4, my IBD got stuck in SMQ2 with the below Error...

Application error: Exception in method EXECUTE of Determ. PDI_HDR_029

Application error: Exception in method EXECUTE of Validation PDI_HDR_013

Message Class:  /BOPF/FRW, number : 7

Can anyone help in resolving this issue....

entry.title: Re: SF Comp Publishing Code - additional conditions in th...
entry.content:
Hi @AlinaTalvescu 

Check out the blog : https://blogs.sap.com/2020/06/11/manage-exclusions-when-publishing-back-to-employee-central/ and if you don't think that the workaround in it will still NOT work for you then unfortunately you will have no other choice but to add custom columns with formula on the worksheet to do the filtering you want.

A classic example is when you have one country out of 50 that requires a publish back to several pay components instead of one. In that case you need to create as many custom columns as there are pay components in EC to publish back to with custom formula (if paycomponent=ABC,finSalary,0) so you can send the right amount to its matching paycomponent in the xml for publish back.

All the best

Xavier

entry.title: Dynamic Parameter Asking for Login Information
entry.content:
Is there a way to prevent Crystal Reports 2020 asking for login information for dynamic parameters?

Our users use the report via a viewer that our ERP system uses. It passes along the login.

entry.title: Avatax Rates by Address in SAP Business One
entry.content:
Is anyone using the native SAP Business One Avatax Rates by Address integration problem free and able to use it through the DIAPI?

We are getting intermittent 'Cannot connect to the Electronic Document Service' errors when using it through the SAP Client and no consistent failures when adding or updating records through the DIAPI.

It falls into the support gap between SAP and Avalara and our SAP Dev Partner has reached the end of his tether trying to diagnose/isolate exactly where/why it fails.

As an aside - it is unable to cope with the full 9 digit USA zip code - it only supports the first 5 characters, but is not smart enough to trim along zip code.

If anyone has this problem or has crossed this bridge, please let get in touch?

grant@delecuona.com

entry.title: Re: Condition Contract for Sales - Manual Delta Accruals
entry.content:
Here’s a revised version of your response:

Hi @Miche1984 ,

Thank you for your detailed question.

To clarify, in standard SAP behavior, when posting a manual delta accrual, there is no need to adjust the Condition Type Group for Accruals, as the system automatically updates the condition type to REA1 in the WCOCOF table. Therefore, including RENT in that group is unnecessary.

Regarding the behavior you’re encountering, it is important to note that standard configuration will not likely meet the expectations you described. The automatic settlement process inherently focuses on business volume transactions/documents. Since your manual delta accrual is an adjustment not tied to any business volume, subsequent automatic settlements (whether delta accrual, partial, or final) will not recognize it as part of the business volume. Instead, it will reverse the manual adjustment and recalculate the correct amount based on the business volume and conditions defined in the contract.

To ensure that the next automatic settlement considers additional adjustments, you could:

  1. Create a Business Volume Document: Generate a document that represents the required adjustment and can be treated as business volume for the contract. This approach allows the automatic settlement to calculate the adjusted amount correctly.

  2. Use a Rebate Adjustment Condition Type (e.g., REJ1): Add a rebate adjustment condition directly in the contract to reflect the adjustment. However, keep in mind that maintaining this condition can be resource-intensive if frequent adjustments are required (e.g., weekly or monthly for the same contract).

I hope this helps clarify the system's standard behavior and provides a path forward. Let me know if further assistance is needed!

Best regards,
Gauthier

entry.title: Re: Difference between GR/IR control & GR/IR Indicator in...
entry.content: Hi Sanan, Thanks for the reply. But am looking for GR/IR control in field selection group,not about GR/IR clearing.

entry.title: Re: SAP integration with Avatax Tax system for US
entry.content: Did you get this to work?

entry.title: Re: Difference between GR/IR control & GR/IR Indicator in...
entry.content: The GR/IR control field is crucial for ensuring the "three-way match" in procurement, where the purchase order, goods receipt, and vendor invoice must all agree before payment is authorized

entry.title: Event-Driven Multi-Tenant Architecture on SAP BTP with CAP and SAP Event Mesh
entry.content:
Introduction

One of the key fundamentals of cloud native applications is that they are not monolithic, which means they are composed by a series of microservices – each one performing a very specific task – and those microservices are loosely-coupled - meaning they essentially do not depend on each other to achieve their outcomes.

A common technique to achieve loosely-coupling is to make microservices communicate with each other through what’s called event messaging: an event that is triggered by one service – e.g. creation or updating of a service entity – generates a message that is “published” (or posted if you will) to an “event message broker” which, then, stores it in a “message queue” to be consumed by another services. Such message contains relevant information about the event – e.g. the unique identifier of the entity for which the event was triggered.

An example of open-source message broker is Apache Kafka which handles messages that follow the CloudEvents specification.

SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) offers four different message broker services: SAP Event Mesh, SAP Event Mesh on Integration Suite, SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh and SAP Cloud Application Event Hub. These services also implement the CloudEvents specification. The choice for one of them depends mostly on the characteristics of each use case where event messaging is required.

The Business Problem

Although those services are quite robust, reliable, and resilient in compliance with cutting-edge cloud application standards, they do not offer the capability of being consumed by multi-tenant applications, which means they are not “tenant-aware” like the destination and the authorization and trust management (XSUAA) services, among others.

For a better understanding of the issue, let’s picture the following business scenario: imagine you have two different customers running on different S/4HANA Cloud instances – let’s call them S4-1 and S4-2 - which are publishing event messages (called topics) to one of the message broker services instantiated in a subaccount on SAP BTP.

Now, let’s suppose that same subaccount serves as provider of a multi-tenant application.

Then, you create two different subscriptions of such multi-tenant application – each one in its corresponding consumer subaccount - and you want the first subscription to receive messages from S4-1 and the second from S4-2 – like you do when a subscription consumes APIs from S4-1 and another from S4-2 via the destination service.

As the message brokers from BTP are not tenant-aware - like the destination service -, that scenario is not possible by default.

The Solution

The fact that you have services which are not tenant-aware doesn’t mean that, with some creativity, you cannot overcome such obstacle. You can solve such kind of situation by simply putting a service in-between the non-tenant-aware service and the multi-tenant application and make such service tenant-aware.

A tenant-aware service requires some kind of “tenant registration” to control which tenant should be served on each request. That’s basically the “backbone” of a tenant-aware service, which is then enriched with other multi-tenant capabilities.

In our case we will develop an “event message dispatcher” service which will act as a kind of “hub” that consumes the messages published by “n” different S/4HANA Cloud tenants to a message queue of a client and then, distribute (dispatch) them to their corresponding subscriber (consumer) tenant.

Well, you might ask: “how do I tie an S/4HANA Cloud tenant to a subscriber tenant?”. Simple: if you inspect the CloudEvents specification, you’ll notice that every message must contain the so-called “context attributes”, one of them being the “source” of the event. That attribute must be unique for each message context, meaning that each S/4HANA Cloud tenant has a unique id for messages published when an event is triggered by an action performed on a BO - such Business Partner (e.g. BP created, or BP changed). For example: when a Business Partner is created on S4-1 the event “source” will be different than the “source” of a Business Partner created on S4-2, but always the same for the Business Partner BO (e.g. S4-1 issues source “/default/sap.s4.beh/740613139” whenever a BP is created or changed, whereas S4-2 issues source “/default/sap.s4.beh/740740843” for those same events).

Therefore, we use the source attribute of the message context to tie each S/4HANA Cloud tenant to a corresponding subscriber tenant in a BTP subaccount.

As previously mentioned, a tenant-aware service must provide some kind of “tenant registration” mechanism to control which tenant should be served on each request. In this specific case, the “request” is replaced by the message consumption from the message queue as it’s not coming from the subscriber, but, instead, being “pushed” to it. So, upon creation of a new subscription of the “multi-tenant event message consumer” application, a “registration” endpoint from the message dispatcher is called passing the relevant tenant information to subsequently tie it to a message source. The dispatcher, then, stores that information in its database and uses it to control the distribution of messages to their corresponding tenants according to the message source.

Solution Architecture

Let’s do a deep dive into the solution architecture for a better understanding of the big picture:

  1. An SAP Event Mesh service instance is created and configured in the provider subaccount. This represents the “message client” in which a message queue is created and “subscribed” to message topics from S/4HANA Cloud. Each S/4HANA Cloud tenant is configured to publish topics to the same message client using its “service key” to setup a communication arrangement.
  2. When the messages are received by the message client, they are stored in the subscribed message queue and then, consumed by the event message dispatcher service (which is a CAP Node.js service).
  3. A consumer subaccount creates a subscription of the event message consumer app (which is also a CAP Node.js service with a Fiori Elements UI).
  4. The SaaS Provisioning Service bound to the event message consumer app handles the subscription creation, whilst the Service Manager takes care of the tenant data isolation in SAP HANA Cloud through a specific database schema for each tenant.
  5. The SaaS Provisioning Service, then, invokes the “on subscription” callback endpoint from the event message consumer app which will, in turn, call the tenant registration endpoint from the event message dispatcher service via destination passing the relevant tenant information, which is stored in the dispatcher database (in this architecture, also SAP HANA Cloud). This communication is secured by the Authentication and Trust Management (XSUAA) service.
  6. When the event message dispatcher service consumes a message from the queue it takes the message source and uses it to search the registration database to find the corresponding tenant subscription. When the tenant is registered in the database the source is set to “new-tenant”, which means that this mechanism works on a “first-come-first-served” mode, hence if the dispatcher does not find the source in the database, it takes the first “new-tenant” source registered and ties the incoming source to it. Using the tenant information tied to the message source, the dispatcher calls a “consume endpoint” from the event message consumer app subscription, also via destination, passing the message data. This communication is also secured by the XSUAA service.
  7. When a user of the app accesses a subscription the Application Router is invoked using the subscription URL.
  8. The Application Router authenticates the user via XSUAA service. Authentication is performed by the configured Identity Provider of the subaccount with a SAML (or OpenID) response to XSUAA which, in turn, issues a JWT that’s forwarded to the event message consumer app. The app, after having processed the messages received from the dispatcher, presents the outcomes to the user via UI.

The Solution Prototype

We have developed a prototype implementing the reference architecture above to demonstrate how it works in practice and serve as a starting point for your own even-driven multi-tenant application. The prototype uses the “create” and “change” event message topics from the Business Partner BO, but, of course, the concept can be applied to other BOs as well.

The source code of the prototype is published to this Git Repo. There you’ll find detailed instructions on how to install and deploy it to your own BTP landscape.

The purpose of this blog post is not to go into the details of the code implementation, so, please, feel free to explore the source code as you wish. It’s fully commented and kind of self-explanatory.

In this post we will focus only on the key aspects of the destinations used in the implementation, because it’s there where the real magic lies.

Solution Destinations

The first one is the Event-Dispatcher destination, which is used by the event message consumer app to perform tenant registration:

  1. After you have deployed the event message dispatcher service to Cloud Foundry, you get the service URL from the deployed application and put it here.
  2. Select the XSUAA service binding of the application, view the sensitive data (service key), get the client id and client secret, and put them here.
  3. From the same service key get the authentication URL, append “/oauth/token” to it and put it here.

There is no mystery in this one: as the service is secured by XSUAA we just use the client credentials and the token service URL to authenticate to the registration endpoint and post the tenant data to it. So, as you may have seen in the Git Repo instructions you must first deploy the event message dispatcher service and then create the above destination.

The trick is in the second destination: Event-Consumer used by the event message dispatcher service to dispatch the message to the event message consumer app subscription.

  1. After you have deployed the event message consumer app to Cloud Foundry, you get the service URL from the deployed application and put it here. However, this one will be dynamically replaced by the event message dispatcher service with the tenant URL that’s been provided upon tenant registration.
  2. Select the XSUAA service binding of the application, view the sensitive data (service key), get the client id and client secret, and put them here.
  3. From the same service key get the authentication URL, append “/oauth/token” to it and put it here, but this time, replace the “sub domain” part of it with the word “subDomain”. And here is where the trick lies: this word is also dynamically replaced by the tenant (subaccount) sub domain that’s provided upon tenant registration.

You might be wondering: “why is that? with URL and token service URL being dynamically replaced this destination serves exclusively to securely store the client credentials?”.

Well, it’s basically that… however, it’s necessary because the “tenant id” for subscription authentication and data isolation is encoded in the JWT issued by XSUAA. Therefore, if you keep the provider app URL in the URL and the provider sub domain in the token service URL you’ll be authenticating in the provider and not in the consumer, thus generating a JWT with the provider tenant id instead of the consumer. This would prevent the event message dispatcher to properly dispatch the message to the corresponding tenant and the event consumer app to properly store the information in the corresponding database schema.

Conclusion

After reading through this blog post (and possibly deploying and inspecting the prototype source code) you may have learnt what’s an event-driven cloud solution and how to implement an event-driven multi-tenant architecture.

We hope you have enjoyed the ride and can take advantage of these learnings in a near future.

Thanks for reading!

entry.title: Re: Difference between GR/IR control & GR/IR Indicator in...
entry.content: Hi Sanan,Can you please share a screenshot of the field in the PO from your system.

entry.title: Expert Feedback: Options for changing a credit card liability type within Concur
entry.content:
Hi all, as a Technical Consultant at Concur I setup a lot of credit card feeds for our customers.  One common question that I get is related to updating the liability type on an existing credit card feed.   Specifically, our customers want to know whether they need to purchase a new feed with Concur or whether we can make changes to the existing one they already have.  This can be a complex issue and there are several things to consider.   In this blog post, I will attempt to address this question using the most common scenarios that we see.   

Ok…before we get too deep into the topic, I want to start by defining the different liability types that are available.  Hopefully these will be familiar to you.   I have seen occasions where a customer may use a slightly different name for a liability type, but in the end they always align to one of these.

  • IBIP (Individual Bill Individual Pay) – This type of credit card is very much like an employee’s personal card.  These cards are the employee’s personal liability, and they are responsible for making payments to them whenever a bill comes due.   When this type of card is used in a business environment, it is the employee’s responsibility to expense any business transactions that they made so they can be reimbursed the funds they need to pay the card for those expenses.   Within Concur, a credit card that is setup with an IBIP liability type will trigger a cash reimbursement payable to the employee.
  • IBCP (Individual Bill Company Pay) -  This type of credit card has a shared liability with the company.   The employee will receive the statements for the credit card, but the expectation is that the company will be making payments on the employee’s behalf based on submitted expense reports.   If the employee does not submit expense reports for this type of card, the account will go delinquent, and both the employee and company can be held liable.  
  • CBCP (Company Bill Company Pay) – This type of card is the liability of the company.   The company is responsible for making payments for this type of card monthly.   The company normally makes their payment from a billing statement that is produced by the credit card vendor.   The company will make this payment regardless of whether the employee has submitted expense reports detailing the types of expenses that were incurred.  Most of the time, a company will complete a reconciliation between the amounts paid to the credit card vendor and the amounts submitted on expense reports to ensure that un-expensed transactions do not grow over time. 

Now that we are aligned on the different liability types available, I’ll get back to the original question about changing the liability type of an existing feed.  Here are the four most common scenarios that we see when making a liability type change.  These scenarios apply to all of the liability types mentioned above and the process does not differ based on liability type.

  • The credit card vendor plans to issue new cards to card holders using a new feed.
  • The credit card vendor plans to issue new cards to card holders using the existing feed.
  • The credit card vendor plans to move existing cards into a new feed with Concur.
  • The credit card vendor plans to use the existing cards in the existing feed. 

I have placed these options in order from most desirable to least desirable in terms of following our best practices.   Read on below as I go through each one.

Option 1: The credit card vendor issues new cards to card holders using a new feed.

From a Concur perspective, this is really the best-case scenario.   The issuing of new card accounts opens the door for the assignment of a new payment type in Concur.   The payment type controls the liability type used on the card and hence determines Concur’s processing method for transactions on that given card.

This is the cleanest approach because it allows for a clean cutover between the old liability type and the new one.   All activity under the old liability type would continue to flow through Concur as it normally would and the new transactions with the new liability type will come in on the new feed just like those from a completely different credit card vendor.  Transactions for both the old and new liability type can exist within Concur at the same time without any issue and there is no cleanup or blackout period required.  

This approach is also the cleanest from a reporting standpoint.   Since the payment type name is created and a new feed was added to Concur, you have two additional data points that can be used to separate activity being processed under the old liability type vs the new one.  Yes, there is a relatively small fee associated with the setup of the new feed, but as you read through these options you will see that in most cases this fee is offset by a reduction in overall effort required for the transition.

Option 2:  The credit card vendor issues new cards to card holders using the existing feed.

Like with option 1, the assignment of new card accounts provides an opportunity for Concur to assign a new payment type for the new cards.   However, since the existing feed is going to be re-used a short blackout period is needed to ensure that all transactions made under the old liability type are processed through to Concur before the payment type can be updated on the credit card feed. 

Concur will need to stop the credit card feed and receive confirmation from the bank regarding the name of the first file that contains transactions with the new liability type.   This is critical because the payment type that is setup with the import job needs to be updated, but it can not be updated until the new file is received.   From a processing standpoint, any transaction that refers to the old payment type will continue to carry the old liability type and the new cards received in the first new file will contain the new payment type and its associated new liability type.  

Transactions can be processed within the same expense report because the assignment of a new payment type ensures that Concur processes the new transactions differently from the old ones.   From a reporting standpoint, the new payment type name can be used to distinguish the old liability type from the new one.      

Option 3:  The credit card vendor moves existing cards into a new feed with Concur.

This is really only a viable option if the number of active cards and outstanding credit card transactions in the system is relatively low.   Let me explain. 

Once a transaction has been imported into Concur and added to an expense report that transaction will retain its assigned liability type even if the associated payment type is changed after the fact.   As a result, all of the transactions currently loaded into the system must be pushed through to posting before any new transactions that should carry the new liability type are imported.      

This requirement usually results in a blackout period where the feed is stopped and all new files that should be processed with the new liability type are held back from being imported while the customer works on the cleanup of the old activity.   Care should be taken to ensure that all transactions are cleaned up in the site and the assistance of someone from Concur may be needed to ensure that all transactions are accounted for. 

Once the cleanup is complete, the customer has two options. 

  1. Re-use the existing payment type and just update the liability type.
  2. Create a new payment type with the new liability type to use with the new feed.

Re-using the existing payment type is possible, but is not recommended because it makes it more difficult to identify transactions that were processed with the old liability type vs the new one.  Since a new feed is also being setup under this option, it is possible to incorporate the old or new feed into any reporting that is being done, but the payment type itself is a much more recognizable and widely understood data point than the credit card import feed.

Creating a new payment type makes the reporting piece easier as discussed above but it does come with a price.  Once a card is assigned to a payment type, the only way that it can be changed is through the UI in Concur.   It cannot be updated through an interface.   This means that even if the payment type was updated on the credit card feed, it will not update the existing accounts that have already been assigned a payment type.  This associated manual update is why we would only consider this type of conversion for customers that have less than 1500 credit cards in use.

Option 4: The credit card vendor uses the existing cards in the existing feed. 

This is the least preferred option and the most difficult to carry out.   It is very similar to option three and carries all of the restrictions and downsides mentioned in option three.  Specifically, it should only be considered if the number of cards and outstanding transactions is relatively low.  A blackout and cleanup period will be required and you will lose all ability to accurately report on transactions being processed under the old liability type vs the new liability type unless you go through with making the manual updates associated with setting up a new payment type mentioned above.    

Wrap up:

Ok…so that is it.   I hope you found this blog post useful.   There are some small variations possible on each of the scenarios mentioned above, but I have tried stick with best practices and feel confident that these cover the primary scenarios you will face.  Please note that if you have an open project with Concur, your assigned Project Manager and Technical Consultant can walk you through each step in the process.   We know that there is already a lot of change required on your side when it comes to updating the credit card liability type and we want to help make the transition as easy as possible for you.

entry.title: Re: open same reports with different parameter values
entry.content:
Hi Shlomi,

There's a few problems with the way you allow users to run those reports...

First issue is Cr for Enterprise is going away, have a look at page 17 on this link:

https://d.dam.sap.com/a/ovSp6DP/BI%20Maintenance%20Strategy%202024.pdf?rc=67&inline=true

BI 2025 removed components
We will not deliver the following components with SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025
Components Go to solution
Universe Design Tool (UDT) – .UNV universe Conversion to UNX (IDT) is strongly recommended (Blog)
Data Federator (DF) : Multi-source universes Several SAP Solutions available (Blog)
SAP BusinessObjects Analysis, edition for OLAP (AOLAP) Several SAP Solutions available (Blog)
Live Office (LO) WebI OData for Excel, Analysis Office (Blog)
Crystal Reports for Enterprise (CR4E) CR2020, WebIntelligence (Blog)

I highly recommend opening your CR4E reports in the desktop version of Crystal Reports and begin converting them, note: you can open CR4E reports in Crystal Reports 2020 Designer, SDK's won't open them though so manually is your only option.

Back to your issue, did it ever work the way you/they wanted it to?

How are they setting the new parameter value?

How are they open multiple copies of the report? Example View Report for the basic report or a scheduled instance etc.?

Are you using any SDK's or simply opening the reports by selecting and clicking on Preview?

Need more details of the work flow...

By default opening Reports via the URL has limitations, when they open the same report using multiple tab's it's the same instance so they won't use multiple parameters unless you can prompt per use. By viewing the same instance it's always going to pass the same values, in your case it doesn't seem to be accepting your parameter value in the URL. Look there for debugging the issue first.

Next steps depends on the workflow they are doing...

Don

entry.title: Re: Difference between GR/IR control & GR/IR Indicator in...
entry.content: The GR/IR control field refer to fields in Invoice tab for the PO Line item i.e. Invoice Receipt and GR-Bsd IV.

entry.title: Re-printing a partially confirmed Prod Ord for the remainder of quantity
entry.content:
How do I re-print a partially confirmed Prod Order for only the remainder of the quantity?

Example:  Prod Order for 10x partially confirmed for 8x pcs.  Re ran MRP for the part number and Prod order now shows 2x outstanding.  How do I print the order to complete just the 2x pcs outstanding.

entry.title: Re: Changing the SO date
entry.content: Thank you

entry.title: Re: Re-dating Production Orders
entry.content: Thank you

entry.title: F2100 Error
entry.content:
Hi,

The standard BP partner function in IOC1 is been deleted some how, as a result while creating new customer i am getting the error as , "In table TPAER, the entry IOC1 SH is missing Message no. F2100" .

I tried to Configure this error by following this process (Transaction SM30, enter table TPAER, click Customizing and it will take you to the IMG) but I don’t know what would be the next step. In IMG screen can any please help me solve this error with proper step's by step process. 

so is there any way of adding that deleted SH partner function in to the system.

Thanks & Regards, 

MOHSIN

entry.title: Re: Enhancements in EBS - BADI FIEB_CHANGE_BS_DATA
entry.content:
Hi @JazzF , 
Hm... that sounds strange. Right now I do now have opportunity to test it specifically, but I would suggest to do a bit of debugging and check what happens with the adjusted value and why it is overwritten. Normally, that would be the first step to do. 

On the other hand, I would not suggest deleting anything from the bank statement. This way, you're deleting potentially useful information, which might have some relevancy. If you're requirements is just to adjust the line item text, you can implement your own logic either in FEB00001 or in BADI FIEB_CHANGE_BS_DATA. To do that, you will have to adjust the value in the parameter FEBEP-SGTXT. You have the access to FEBRE-table in the interfaces of both enhancement points, you can read the content and adjust the values flexibly for the line item text where it is needed. 

I had similar requirement on one of the projects. The requirement was a bit simplified. I had to delete from line item text some fixed values and to capitalize all letters. So that's what I did via FEBEP-parameter. 
I hope it helps you with your inquiry !

Regards,

Bohdan

entry.title: Re: Issue with Employee History Salary Field for Hourly P...
entry.content: Yes it is also relevant for Compensation. @Sarnendu_Chatte

entry.title: Amount field length extension in S4HC
entry.content:
Dear Community, 

Is it possible to extend the length of amount fields?

There a blogs about this feature in S4H, but I do not see the equivalent for S4H Cloud. 

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/amount-field-length-extension-afle-limitations-in-sap-s-4-hana/ba-p/13444049

Thank you,

Kind regards

Anaïs

entry.title: Reported no show status replication error in ECP
entry.content:
Receiving the error when trying to replicate a no- show status from EC to ECP

Program condition violated(\CLASS=CX_HRPA_VIOLATED_ASSERTION)

entry.title: Re: Event Mesh - limit of 32767 reached
entry.content: Did you ever find a way around this?

entry.title: ERROR while doing GR
entry.content:
IAM trying to inward the material in migo through GR . It shows error

Period 009/2024 is not open for account type S and G/L 896000

 

regards

  Vijay

entry.title: I like to create a SAP Blog

entry.title: Re: Deep Link for Completed Forms goes to Inbox
entry.content:
Hi @hgutschmidt 

I just tested in my demo and the system is giving me the same behavior than you (takes me to main inbox instead of completed folder).

However it is still listed as an available deep link in the latest (post 2H 2024) documentation:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_PLATFORM/5c6733335c434d6f81210378bbea6405/6d149a28c77a4f3da92fdc0f5dde9079.html?q=deep+link

I would create an incident for this so our engineers can advise on what is happening.

Thank you

All the best

Xavier

entry.title: Re: Enhanced Error Handling within SAP Digital Manufacturing Production Processes

entry.title: Re: how to change the font color of Label
entry.content: Very Good!

entry.title: Re: Condition Contract for Sales - Manual Delta Accruals
entry.content:
Dear Gauthier,

First of all, I would like to sincerely thank you for the assistance you’ve provided. We are facing significant challenges with this new feature.

Based on you feedback, our scenario appears to be a use case not covered by the standard functionality, despite it being a common request among users across various industries (posting accruals without quantities that must be reversed during the final settlement run).

I have some additional questions and comments:

1) Regarding the first method "Create a Business Volume Document". Could you clarify what type of document we can be created? Are you suggesting a sort of "fake" billing document that artificially increases the business volume through reported quantities?

1) For the secondo method, "Use a Rebate Adjustment Condition Type (e.g., REJ1)" I guess it can be applied in the data migration phase: we can create a condition contract that includes an REJ1 condition "to offset" the accruals that we'll post through WB2R_DOC_ENTRY to migrate accruals recorded in SAP ECC).

Thank you again for your support, and I look forward to your guidance on this matter.

Best regards,
Michele

Did you find a way to do this? I need to auto populate some fields from the Compensation portlet to the custom MDF object that I created and I am not able to create a rule for that.

 

Thank you.

Bets Regards,

Joana Dourado

entry.title: Re: How to master a successful on-premise H4S4 Migration: SAP ERP6 HCM to SAP HCM for S/4HANA (H4S4)
entry.content: Muito boa a orientação!

entry.title: Display percentage for a calculated measure in Datasphere
entry.content:
Solution:

Assigning percentage to the calculated measure can be done at Datasphere or in the SAC story.

In Datasphere Fact View

The calculated measure can be displayed as percentage by following the steps given below in Datasphere

In the datasphere fact model create a calculated measure and calculate the percentage.

Create a calculated column  and assign '%' as constant value

 

Changes the semantics for the calculated column . Choose Unit of Measure  as semantic type

For the percentage calculated Column , choose the semantic type as 'Quantity with Unit' and  assign  the calculated column created in step 3.

 

Create analytical model using the fact table. 

Create a story in SAC using the datasphere analytical model. Use the calculated column in chart or table.

calculated measure displays the percentage in the widgets.

In SAC story

Similarly in SAC , if there is a use case to display % for a calculated measure, follow the steps given below

  1. Create a calculated measure in SAC story on the Datasphere analytic model.
  2. Call the calculated key figure that is created in Datasphere into the calculated measure created in SAC.
  3. Go to the formatting of the SAC calculated measure that is created and select the options as shown below.
  • Uncheck the option “Use unit of underlying measure’ and chose “Percentage” as Scale.
  • Provide ‘0’ in decimal places.(optional or choose as per the requirement)

  • Remove the actual key figure from the story .
  • Select the calculated measure on the story and change the styling option as shown below

  • Calculated Measure will display as % in the story.

It is preferred to format the measure at the source if it has to be used at different stories instead of recreating again.

entry.title: Date picker unresponsive in SAP Mobile Start (but works well in desktop browser)
entry.content:
Dear experts,

In our app there is an oData function import with an import parameter of type date. When the action is initiated, an input field is rendered with datepicker value help, and it opens the usual calendar and clockface dialog:

This works perfectly in the desktop browsers but on mobile devices via SAP Start there is a strange malfunction:

  • the first time the action popup is opened, date picker works normally, i.e. as in screenshots above
  • but if the user performs following sequence:
    1. picks a date
    2. then cancels the action
    3. then reopens the action again,

        the date picker dialog no longer appears. The datepicker icon is seemingly activated when touched (it briefly flickers in colour when you press), but it is unresponsive.

  • if the action is cancelled again and the page is refreshed, everything is again back to normal.

This happens on both Android and iOS.

Please suggest what could be the problem, or how to analyse further. I am currently trying to remote-debug it on Android and I will post any interesting findings.

Update: in remote debugging this comes up:

adding element with duplicate id 'ActionUtil-BLAHBLAHDIBLAH_MY_SERVICE_Entities-<My_Action_Name>-<My_Parameter_Name>-input-PC' -

Thank you, 

BR Petra

entry.title: how can we change global uuid to customer value in IAS user store
entry.content:
We are trying to use our own customer ID for the global uuid within the IAS user record in the Identity Directory as described in this SAP doc: Global User ID in Integration Scenarios | SAP Help Portal. 

In our solution, users are provisioned via IPS from SuccessFactors and we want this to include a customer value for the global uuid, but the updates we have made to the transformations for the IPS SCIM API read of SuccessFactors as source (IAS as target) as described in the document have not worked.

Has anyone successfully deployed their own customer value into global uuid in IAS in this way who has any insight?

Regards

Mariano

2)Any documentation around maintaining contract attributes with major columns and its usages?

entry.title: Request for Recommendations on Upgrading from SLES 12 to SLES 15 SP6
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

We are planning to upgrade our Linux operating system from SLES 12 to SLES 15 SP6. This operating system currently hosts only the database.

Our kernel version is:

  • Version: 777
  • Patch: 500

Our database version is as follows:

  • SAP HANA 1.0, 2.0
  • Version: 2.00.052.00.1599235305
  • DBSL: 777.05
  • Patch: 500

Could anyone share any recommendations or best practices to ensure a smooth upgrade process? Specifically, are there any SAP-specific considerations or potential issues we should be aware of when performing this OS upgrade?

Thank you in advance for your help!

Best regards,

Heitor Lima

entry.title: Re: U.S Tax Reporter Year End 2024
entry.content:
Hello community,

SAP notes released:
3548310 – TR: Vermont Child Care Contribution in W-2/W-2c Forms Box 14 and Electronic Files
3550830 – TR: Q4/2024 functional changes for U.S. Tax Reporter

Should be available by the end of the day:
3551406 – TR: Year End 2024 Phase III for U.S. Tax Reporter

Happy Holidays!
Kind regards,
Graziela

entry.title: Re: U.S Tax Reporter Year End 2024
entry.content: @rosette_vaughn2 As far as I know they will be releasing the changes in Regulatory Bulletin 156.

entry.title: Re: Tracking field level changes done to database tables using Se16N
entry.content:
First of all, I would like to thank you for your response.

If the saved records contain the information before it was modified (old), this means that we do not have the information as it was (new), do you agree? We can even make a comparison with later records, however, between one change and another, the information may have been changed in the system and we will not have the exact reference (new).

Do you agree?

entry.title: Re: I like to create a SAP Blog
entry.content:
Solved: Create a Blog button is Disabled. How to enable it... - SAP Community

NB: before posting, make sure that someone else didn't create a blog post on the same topic.

Thank you for the help.

Could you pls provide me more if anything you can do and also for Delete operation?

entry.title: Re: Attachments in mobile services Can anyone help me?
entry.content: You should check with the service provider for documentation on what that POST call is expecting.

entry.title: Innovation Day 2024 Recap and Happy Holidays
entry.content:
It may be a little late to go back to an event that we already did at the beginning of November. But that does not mean it is not totally worth it. Approaching the end of the year it is always worthwhile to take a step back and look at everything that has happened. But this time I want to look back even a little bit further. SAP Women in Tech has already existed for more than 6 years now. Throughout this time, we have been able to build an incredible network, have seen countless amazing speakers and put together our fair share of well-received events.

This November we finally put everything together with a full day event including a total of 15 sessions and 22 speakers: Our SAP Women in Tech Innovation Day 2024. If I had to describe it with one sentence I would probably say: “The best of both worlds”. Meaning we combined the two strongest topics that we have been working on for the past years. Technology and helping to upskill female speakers and putting them on stages. Needless to say, that with this powerful combination we had a huge turnout and countless attendees that day. It was overall the biggest on-site event we have ever done for SAP Women in Tech and the feedback was incredibly positive.

Looking now towards 2025 one thing is for sure. There is going to be another Innovation Day and we will continue to upskill our female speakers at SAP so that eventually, we will see enough of them on stages across all areas.

All that is left to say is Happy Holidays to everyone reading this and we will see you next year!

entry.title: Re: Payroll Canada Year End 2024
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm not a tax expert, so I'm hoping someone can help me out with this.

This is in our QAS system. 

We have some employees moving from Province of Employment QC to ON effective Jan. 1. Infotypes 0461, 0462 and 0463 have been updated effective Jan 1. 

Pay period 01-2025 runs from 21.12.2024 to 03.01.2025 with pay date on 10.01.2025. When we run PP01, it is still calculating QC tax, QPP, QPIP etc. It also has an amount for CPP. If taxes etc. are calculated "when paid" shouldn't the QC stuff no longer be there? 

Am I missing anything?

Thanks in advance for your advice.

Elizabeth

entry.title: once you run AJRW can we not post any further transactions in the old fiscal year
entry.content:
Hi team 

1. I have run ARJW today and I wanted to the consequences further in the next fiscal year or in the same year. Will there be any impact on the assets or effect on any postings?

2. once you run AJRW can we not post any further transactions in the old fiscal year?

3. if there is any impact on business, is there any tcode to reverse it?

Please provide your valuable suggestions

 

Regards

Jithendra Shivashankar

entry.title: Re: Desvincular RC de um contrato
entry.content:
ME53N

Use a transação ME53N

No outro contrato deve possuir o mesmo número do material;

Coloque o nº do contrato e o nº do item equivalente;

Confira o nº do fornecedor, o Centro.

entry.title: How to transport SAP Build Process Automation project from Dev subaccount to quality subaccount
entry.content:
Hello Gurus,

Is it possible to transport SAP Build Process Automation Project from Dev subaccount to quality Subaccount by exporting the project as .mtar file and importing the same into quality subaccount.

when i tried the same by importing to quality subaccount, the definition id still pointing to the dev subaccount.

Regards,

Naveen Kishore

entry.title: EWM Packspec create with 0 Qty
entry.content:
Hi Experts

Is there a way to make below value 0 in packspec, user will decide qty at the time of receipt.
